ddls:
CREATE TABLE "sd_cards" (
  "card_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each SD card */,
  "card_type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the card type (e.g., SD Memory Card, SD ROM Card, OTP) */,
  "bus_width" TEXT /* Currently defined data bus width for the card */,
  "secured_mode" INTEGER /* Whether the card is in secured mode or not */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the SD card */,
  "manufacturer" TEXT /* Manufacturer of the SD card */,
  "production_date" TEXT /* Production date of the SD card */,
  "expiration_date" TEXT /* Expiration date of the SD card */,
  "serial_number" TEXT /* Serial number of the SD card */,
  "storage_capacity" TEXT /* Storage capacity of the SD card */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("card_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sd_cards_bus_width FOREIGN KEY ("bus_width") REFERENCES bus_widths ("bus_width"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sd_cards_card_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("card_type_id") REFERENCES card_types ("card_type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sd_cards_secured_mode FOREIGN KEY ("secured_mode") REFERENCES secured_modes ("secured_mode")
)

CREATE TABLE "card_types" (
  "card_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each card type */,
  "card_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the card type (e.g., SD Memory Card, SD ROM Card, OTP) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the card type */,
  "standard_compliance" TEXT /* Standard compliance of the card type */,
  "compatibility" TEXT /* Compatibility of the card type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("card_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "bus_widths" (
  "bus_width_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each bus width */,
  "bus_width" TEXT /* Data bus width (e.g., 1, 4) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the bus width */,
  "transfer_rate" TEXT /* Transfer rate of the bus width */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("bus_width_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "secured_modes" (
  "secured_mode_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each secured mode */,
  "secured_mode" INTEGER /* Whether the card is in secured mode or not */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the secured mode */,
  "security_level" TEXT /* Security level of the secured mode */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("secured_mode_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reserved_bits" (
  "reserved_bit_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each reserved bit */,
  "bit_position" INTEGER /* Position of the reserved bit */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the reserved bit */,
  "usage" TEXT /* Usage of the reserved bit */,
  "reserved_by" TEXT /* Reserved by (e.g., manufacturer, standard) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("reserved_bit_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sd_card_settings" (
  "setting_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each setting */,
  "card_id" INTEGER /* ID of the SD card */,
  "bus_width_id" INTEGER /* ID of the bus width */,
  "secured_mode_id" INTEGER /* ID of the secured mode */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the setting */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* User ID of the creator */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Creation date of the setting */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("setting_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sd_card_settings_card_id FOREIGN KEY ("card_id") REFERENCES sd_cards ("card_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sd_card_settings_secured_mode_id FOREIGN KEY ("secured_mode_id") REFERENCES secured_modes ("secured_mode_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sd_card_settings_bus_width_id FOREIGN KEY ("bus_width_id") REFERENCES bus_widths ("bus_width_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., engineer, quality assurance, administrator) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user */,
  "job_title" TEXT /* Job title of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role FOREIGN KEY ("role") REFERENCES roles ("role_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "card_id" INTEGER /* ID of the SD card being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the SD card */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the SD card was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_card_id FOREIGN KEY ("card_id") REFERENCES sd_cards ("card_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "card_type_descriptions" (
  "description_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each description */,
  "card_type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the card type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the card type */,
  "features" TEXT /* Features of the card type */,
  "benefits" TEXT /* Benefits of the card type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("description_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_card_type_descriptions_card_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("card_type_id") REFERENCES card_types ("card_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "bus_width_descriptions" (
  "description_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each description */,
  "bus_width_id" INTEGER /* ID of the bus width */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the bus width */,
  "bandwidth" TEXT /* Bandwidth of the bus width */,
  "speed" TEXT /* Speed of the bus width */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("description_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_bus_width_descriptions_bus_width_id FOREIGN KEY ("bus_width_id") REFERENCES bus_widths ("bus_width_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "secured_mode_descriptions" (
  "description_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each description */,
  "secured_mode_id" INTEGER /* ID of the secured mode */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the secured mode */,
  "security_features" TEXT /* Security features of the secured mode */,
  "protection_level" TEXT /* Protection level of the secured mode */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("description_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_secured_mode_descriptions_secured_mode_id FOREIGN KEY ("secured_mode_id") REFERENCES secured_modes ("secured_mode_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reserved_bit_descriptions" (
  "description_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each description */,
  "reserved_bit_id" INTEGER /* ID of the reserved bit */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the reserved bit */,
  "purpose" TEXT /* Purpose of the reserved bit */,
  "usage" TEXT /* Usage of the reserved bit */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("description_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reserved_bit_descriptions_reserved_bit_id FOREIGN KEY ("reserved_bit_id") REFERENCES reserved_bits ("reserved_bit_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sd_card_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each version */,
  "card_id" INTEGER /* ID of the SD card */,
  "version_number" TEXT /* Version number of the SD card */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Release date of the SD card version */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the SD card version */,
  "changes" TEXT /* Changes in the SD card version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sd_card_versions_card_id FOREIGN KEY ("card_id") REFERENCES sd_cards ("card_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "card_type_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each version */,
  "card_type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the card type */,
  "version_number" TEXT /* Version number of the card type */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Release date of the card type version */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the card type version */,
  "changes" TEXT /* Changes in the card type version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_card_type_versions_card_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("card_type_id") REFERENCES card_types ("card_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "bus_width_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each version */,
  "bus_width_id" INTEGER /* ID of the bus width */,
  "version_number" TEXT /* Version number of the bus width */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Release date of the bus width version */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the bus width version */,
  "changes" TEXT /* Changes in the bus width version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_bus_width_versions_bus_width_id FOREIGN KEY ("bus_width_id") REFERENCES bus_widths ("bus_width_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role (e.g., engineer, quality assurance, administrator) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id", "role_id")
)
